thresholdComparisons.threshold: corpus-size-docs is not statically observable; decided on feature-signal-count instead

rationale: Consolidate elasticsearch:elasticsearch — corpus-size-docs isn't statically observable in this repo, so the verdict is decided on the observable proxy instead: feature-signal-count = 0. Evidence: src/search.ts:6.

migrationEffort.rollbackNote: The search index is derived data — drop the generated column/index and re-point queries at the old search cluster, which stays available (read-only) until the cutover is verified.

thresholdComparisons.threshold: est-ops-per-sec is not statically observable; decided on command-mix-plain-kv-share instead

rationale: Consolidate redis:redis — est-ops-per-sec isn't statically observable in this repo, so the verdict is decided on the observable proxy instead: command-mix-plain-kv-share = 1. Evidence: src/cache.ts:7.

migrationEffort.rollbackNote: Cache contents are derived/ephemeral — no rollback needed, point reads back at Redis and let the UNLOGGED table go cold.

thresholdComparisons.threshold: value < 1,000 msgs/sec | 1,000 <= value < 10,000 msgs/sec | value >= 10,000 msgs/sec

rationale: Consolidate redis:redis — est-peak-msgs-sec observed 2-200 msgs/sec vs value < 1,000 msgs/sec | 1,000 <= value < 10,000 msgs/sec | value >= 10,000 msgs/sec (independent: https://medium.com/@the_atomic_architect/postgresql-replaced-my-message-queue-and-taught-me-skip-locked-along-the-way-87d59e5b9525). Evidence: package.json:14.
postgresEquivalent: Plain SQL, FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED

migrationEffort.rollbackNote: Same dual-write cutover as pgmq: drain the old broker before retiring it, revert by re-pointing producers if needed.
